title: De-jargon every internal-tracker-ID citation in pi-behavior-monitors's code comments (FGAP-136 rollout)
description: Rewrite every code comment in packages/pi-behavior-monitors (this package has no src/ subdirectory -- its code lives at the package root: index.ts, index.test.ts, auth-required.ts) that cites an internal .context-tracker ID (FEAT-NNN, TASK-NNN, DEC-NNNN, FGAP-NNN, JI-NNN) to state the same substantive point in plain English instead, regardless of whether the cited ID still resolves correctly -- opacity to a reader outside this project is the defect, per FGAP-136. Depends on TASK-109 merging first. The implementing agent works from a replacement-text table produced by a dedicated Explore pass over this package's citations (grep of index.ts and index.test.ts, since scripts/scan-comment-citations.ts's package-discovery step correctly lists this package but its file-walk found nothing under a nonexistent src/ path -- the tool needs no fix, this task's own filed text originally did), not by re-deriving meaning itself.
acceptance_criteria: Every internal-tracker-ID citation found in packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts and index.test.ts is replaced with plain-English prose stating the same substantive point, grounded in recovered meaning -- never fabricated.
A direct grep of packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts and index.test.ts for the same ID patterns shows zero remaining instances.
npm run build + npm run check + npm test exit 0.
